title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Type-System Trace: Negative Type Tests
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts negative type tests increment in a behavior-and-type regression suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.
Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Separate compile-time claims from runtime facts, narrow every external unknown with executable evidence, inspect diagnostics and emitted JavaScript, then test a changed schema without assertions or suppression. Apply that move specifically to Negative Type Tests while producing a behavior-and-type regression suite.
